title: malpighia urens subsp. ovatifolia
intro: Malpighia urens subsp. ovatifolia, also known as the 'shrubby mallow' or 'wild arbol de pan', is a plant species belonging to the Malpighiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Malpighia urens subsp. ovatifolia include the potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
